name: workflow-plugin-data-engineering
description: Data engineering plugin: CDC, lakehouse, time-series, graph, data quality, catalog, migration, multi-tenancy
author: GoCodeAlone

stepSchemas.description: Start a CDC source stream. Looks up the cdc.source module by source_id and establishes the change stream.

configFields.description: Unique identifier for the CDC source to start
Comma-separated list of tables to capture

stepSchemas.description: Stop a running CDC source stream and deregister it.

configFields.description: Unique identifier for the CDC source to stop

stepSchemas.description: Get the current status of a running CDC stream.

configFields.description: Unique identifier for the CDC source

outputs.description: Timestamp of last received event (if available)

stepSchemas.description: Trigger a full table snapshot on a running CDC stream.

configFields.description: Unique identifier for the CDC source
List of tables to snapshot (empty = all tracked tables)

stepSchemas.description: Query DDL change history for a specific table on a CDC source.

configFields.description: Unique identifier for the CDC source
Table name to query DDL history for

outputs.description: List of schema versions with table, version, ddl, applied_at fields

stepSchemas.description: Evaluate CDC lag and optionally throttle or resume a stream based on configurable thresholds.

configFields.description: Unique identifier for the CDC source
Lag bytes threshold for critical status (0 = disabled)
Lag seconds threshold for critical status (0 = disabled)
Fraction of threshold at which to emit a warning (default 0.8)

outputs.description: Backpressure status: healthy, warning, or critical

stepSchemas.description: Collect and return health metrics for a CDC source stream.

configFields.description: Unique identifier for the CDC source

outputs.description: Whether the source is currently healthy

stepSchemas.description: Provision a new tenant namespace (schema, database, or row-level based on strategy).

configFields.description: Unique identifier for the tenant to provision
Database connection URL for the target database

stepSchemas.description: Archive or delete a tenant namespace.

configFields.description: Unique identifier for the tenant to deprovision

stepSchemas.description: Run schema migrations for one or more tenants.

configFields.description: Tenant identifier to migrate (or 'all' for all tenants)
Number of tenants to migrate concurrently

stepSchemas.description: Evaluate whether a tenant meets the criteria for strategy promotion.

configFields.description: Unique identifier for the tenant to evaluate

outputs.description: Whether the tenant is eligible for promotion

stepSchemas.description: Promote a tenant from a shared strategy to a dedicated strategy.

configFields.description: Unique identifier for the tenant to promote

stepSchemas.description: Demote a tenant from a dedicated strategy back to a shared strategy.

configFields.description: Unique identifier for the tenant to demote

stepSchemas.description: Create a new table in the Iceberg lakehouse catalog.

configFields.description: Name of the catalog.iceberg module to use
Iceberg schema definition with fields array
Object storage location for table data

outputs.description: Storage location of the created table

stepSchemas.description: Apply schema evolution changes to an existing Iceberg table.

configFields.description: Name of the catalog.iceberg module to use
List of schema change objects (add_column, drop_column, rename_column)

stepSchemas.description: Write records to an Iceberg lakehouse table.

configFields.description: Name of the catalog.iceberg module to use
Template expression to source records from step outputs

outputs.description: ID of the snapshot created by this write

stepSchemas.description: Compact small data files in an Iceberg table into larger ones.

configFields.description: Name of the catalog.iceberg module to use
Target output file size in megabytes

stepSchemas.description: Create a named snapshot of an Iceberg table for time-travel or restore.

configFields.description: Name of the catalog.iceberg module to use

stepSchemas.description: Execute a SQL query against an Iceberg lakehouse catalog.

configFields.description: Name of the catalog.iceberg module to use
Maximum number of rows to return
Snapshot ID or timestamp for time-travel queries

outputs.description: Query result rows as array of objects

stepSchemas.description: Expire old Iceberg table snapshots to reclaim storage space.

configFields.description: Name of the catalog.iceberg module to use
Table name to expire snapshots from
Expire snapshots older than this duration (e.g. 168h for 7 days)
Minimum number of snapshots to retain regardless of age

outputs.description: Number of orphan data files deleted

stepSchemas.description: Write a single time-series data point to the configured time-series module.

configFields.description: Name of the timeseries module to write to
Data point timestamp (RFC3339 or Unix nanoseconds; empty = now)

stepSchemas.description: Write a batch of time-series data points to the configured time-series module.

configFields.description: Name of the timeseries module to write to
Array of data point objects (each with measurement, tags, fields, timestamp)

stepSchemas.description: Execute a time-series query (Flux, SQL, or InfluxQL) against the configured module.

configFields.description: Name of the timeseries module to query
Query string (Flux, SQL, or InfluxQL depending on backend)
Query start time (RFC3339 or relative e.g. -1h)
Query end time (RFC3339 or relative; empty = now)

stepSchemas.description: Downsample high-resolution time-series data into a lower-resolution summary table.
Apply a retention policy to delete data older than the specified duration.

configFields.description: Measurement to apply retention to (empty = all)
Delete data older than this duration (e.g. 720h for 30 days)

stepSchemas.description: Create or update a continuous query / scheduled task that auto-downsamples data.

configFields.description: Aggregation query to run on schedule

stepSchemas.description: Archive old time-series data to a cold tier (object storage or separate table).

configFields.description: Archive data older than this duration
Destination object storage bucket or table prefix

outputs.description: Number of rows moved to archive

stepSchemas.description: Return the storage tier breakdown (hot/warm/cold) for a time-series module.

outputs.description: Number of rows in hot tier
Number of rows in warm tier
Number of rows in cold/archive tier

stepSchemas.description: Create or replace a ClickHouse MaterializedView for continuous aggregation.

configFields.description: Name of the materialized view to create
SELECT query for the materialized view

stepSchemas.description: Submit a Druid batch or streaming ingestion task.
Execute a Druid SQL or native query.

configFields.description: Druid SQL or native query JSON

stepSchemas.description: Create or update a Druid datasource specification.
Trigger a Druid compaction task for a datasource to optimize segment storage.

configFields.description: ISO-8601 interval to compact (e.g. 2024-01-01/2024-02-01; empty = all)

stepSchemas.description: Register a new schema version in the Schema Registry.

configFields.description: Schema definition (Avro JSON, JSON Schema, or Protobuf IDL)

stepSchemas.description: Validate a payload against a schema version in the Schema Registry.

configFields.description: Schema version to validate against (number or 'latest')
Data to validate (inline JSON or template expression)

outputs.description: Whether the data is valid against the schema

stepSchemas.description: Execute a Cypher or Gremlin query against the graph database.
Write nodes and/or relationships to the graph database.

configFields.description: Nodes to create or merge (each with labels and properties)
Relationships to create or merge (each with type, from_id, to_id, properties)

stepSchemas.description: Bulk-import nodes and relationships from a structured data source into the graph database.

configFields.description: Source data: inline JSON, file path, or template expression
Number of nodes/relationships per transaction batch

stepSchemas.description: Extract named entities from text using rule-based NLP and write them to the graph database.

configFields.description: Input text to extract entities from
Entity types to extract (e.g. PERSON, ORG, LOCATION)

outputs.description: Extracted entities with type, text, and span

stepSchemas.description: Extract named entities from text using an LLM and write them to the graph database.

configFields.description: Input text to extract entities from
Custom system prompt for entity extraction

outputs.description: Extracted entities with type, text, and confidence

stepSchemas.description: Create a typed relationship between two existing graph nodes.

configFields.description: Source node ID or lookup expression
Target node ID or lookup expression

stepSchemas.description: Register a data asset (dataset, pipeline, table) in the data catalog.

configFields.description: Name of the catalog module (datahub or openmetadata)

stepSchemas.description: Search the data catalog for assets matching a query.

configFields.description: Name of the catalog module (datahub or openmetadata)
Filter by entity type (dataset, pipeline, dashboard)
Maximum number of results to return

stepSchemas.description: Register an upstream/downstream lineage relationship between two data assets.

configFields.description: Description of the transformation between source and target

stepSchemas.description: Query upstream or downstream lineage for a data asset.

configFields.description: Entity URN or ID to query lineage for

stepSchemas.description: Validate a data payload against a YAML or JSON data contract.

configFields.description: Name of the catalog module or quality.checks module
Data to validate (inline JSON or template expression)

outputs.description: Whether the data satisfies all contract terms
List of contract violations (empty if valid)

stepSchemas.description: Run data quality checks (not-null, range, uniqueness, custom SQL) against a table or dataset.

configFields.description: Table or dataset name to check
Array of quality rule objects (type, column, threshold)
Fraction of failed checks that triggers pipeline failure (0.0–1.0)

stepSchemas.description: Validate the live schema of a table against an expected schema definition.

configFields.description: Expected schema definition (columns with name and type)

outputs.description: Whether the live schema matches the expected schema

stepSchemas.description: Generate a statistical data profile for a table (row count, nulls, min/max, cardinality).

configFields.description: Sample fraction for large tables (0.0–1.0; 1.0 = full scan)

outputs.description: Per-column statistics (null_count, min, max, cardinality, mean)

stepSchemas.description: Compare data quality profiles between two tables or datasets (e.g. source vs. target).

outputs.description: Whether both datasets are statistically equivalent

stepSchemas.description: Detect anomalies in time-series or tabular data using statistical methods.

configFields.description: Anomaly sensitivity multiplier (default 3.0, lower = more sensitive)

outputs.description: List of detected anomaly points with timestamp and value

stepSchemas.description: Run dbt tests for a specified model and report results.

outputs.description: Per-test results with name and status

stepSchemas.description: Run Soda data quality checks defined in a checks YAML file.

configFields.description: Table to run Soda checks against
List of Soda check definitions (or path to checks YAML)

outputs.description: Per-check results with outcome and details

stepSchemas.description: Run a Great Expectations validation suite against a table or batch.

configFields.description: Table or batch name to validate

stepSchemas.description: Generate a migration plan by diffing current and desired schema states.

configFields.description: Current schema state (auto-detected from target if omitted)
Desired schema state or migration file

outputs.description: Generated migration plan with operations list
Whether the plan contains breaking changes
List of migration operations (add_column, drop_column, etc.)

stepSchemas.description: Apply a previously generated migration plan to the target database.

configFields.description: When true, validate the plan without executing it

stepSchemas.description: Run pending SQL migration scripts from the migrations directory up to an optional target version.

configFields.description: Migration version to run to (empty = latest)
When true, list pending migrations without executing

stepSchemas.description: Roll back the last applied migration or roll back to a specific version.

configFields.description: Version to roll back to (empty = roll back one step)

stepSchemas.description: Return the current migration state including applied, pending, and current version.
Perform the expand phase of an expand-contract migration: add new columns/tables without removing old ones.

configFields.description: List of backward-compatible additions (add_column, add_index, add_table)

stepSchemas.description: Perform the contract phase of an expand-contract migration: remove old columns/tables after all consumers have migrated.

configFields.description: List of removals (drop_column, drop_index, drop_table) to finalize

stepSchemas.description: Check the status of an in-progress expand-contract migration.

outputs.description: Current phase: expand, transition, or contract
Whether all consumers have migrated and contraction is safe

stepSchemas.description: Execute a full automated schema evolution pipeline (detect drift, plan, expand, notify consumers, contract).

configFields.description: Evolution pipeline configuration name or inline spec
Automatically apply non-breaking changes without manual approval

stepSchemas.description: Verify that a schema evolution pipeline completed successfully and all consumers are on the new schema.

configFields.description: Evolution pipeline configuration name to verify

outputs.description: Whether all consumers are on the new schema
Consumers still on the old schema (if any)
